revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ishinohōden Kofun]][[ja:石宝殿古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Neyagawa, Kansai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ishinohōden Kofun | native_name =石宝殿古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ishinohōden Kofun, front view | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ishinohōden Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.752478|N|135.658478|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Neyagawa, Osaka|en|Neyagawa, Osaka|qid=Q389633|lt=Neyagawa, Osaka|1=Neyagawa, Osaka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|45|8.92|N|135|39|30.52|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ishinohōden Kofun'''|石宝殿古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Uchiage Motomachi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Neyagawa, Osaka|en|Neyagawa, Osaka|qid=Q389633|lt=Neyagawa, Osaka|1=Neyagawa, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1973.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1820 |title=石宝殿古墳 |trans-title=Ishinohōden Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ishinohōden Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located an elevation of 100 meters, on a gently sloping hill at the western foot of {{ill|Mount Ikoma|en|Mount Ikoma|qid=Q996259|lt=Mount Ikoma|1=Mount Ikoma}}. The mound has been completely eroded away, leaving behind the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a base stone of 2.7 meters by 1.6 meters and a ceiling stone of 3.2 meters by 3.3 meters. It is highly unusual in that it is made of two monoliths of granite which have been hollowed out to form a room inside, rather than being made stacked slabs of stones. The interior is 0.9 meters wide, 0.8 meters high and 2.2 meters deep, and the entrance is 0.5 meters wide. There are round dents on the upper and lower surfaces on the left side of the entrance, and it is believed that these were a door. Two more large megaliths are arranged outside to form an entry portico, orientated to the south. The ''kofun'' is located on the grounds of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, the Kora Jinja.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Three megaliths are lined up in a row behind the tumulus, and an archaeological survey conducted in 1988 confirmed that more stones continue underground to the west. The angle of these stones is 135 degrees, and if these correspond to the outer line of the Kofun, the configuration of the tumulus may have been octagonal.
This tumulus is presumed to have been built around the middleof the 7th century (at the end of the Kofun period) from a small fragment of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery which was found at the site, and is believed to have been an octagonal-shaped structure. It is mentioned in [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] records, but no modern [[archaeological excavation]]s or surveys have been conducted. As the burial chamber has been open for centuries, there are no documented {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}; however, [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] documents indicate that a gold-copper burial vessel containing white bones was excavated from near the tumulus. Its whereabouts are now unknown.
The site is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Neyagawakōen Station|en|Neyagawakōen Station|qid=Q855863|lt=Neyagawakōen Station|1=Neyagawakōen Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Katamachi Line|en|Katamachi Line|qid=Q1073379|lt=Katamachi Line|1=Katamachi Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|Side View File:Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Entry to the Burial Chamber File:Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Inside the Burial Chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{template-inline|Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun}} *[https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378194459718.html Neyagawa City home page] {{in lang|ja}} ]]
{{Kofun navbar}}
